back_translation: A prayer of David. Hear, O LORD, a righteous plea; give heed to my cry; listen to my prayer from lips free of deceit.
Let my vindication come from Your presence; let Your eyes see what is right.
You have tested my heart; You have visited me in the night; You have refined me and found nothing evil; I have resolved that my mouth will not transgress.
As for the deeds of men—by the word of Your lips I have avoided the ways of the violent.
My steps have held firmly to Your paths; my feet have not slipped.
O God, I call on You, for You will answer me; hear my words, and incline Your ear to me.
Show the wonders of Your steadfast love, You who save those who take refuge from their foes, by Your right hand.
Keep me as the apple of the eye; hide me in the shadow of Your wings,
from the wicked who assail me, from my mortal enemies who surround me.
They have closed their hardened hearts; their mouths speak with arrogance.
Now they surround our steps; they fix their eyes to cast us to the ground,
like a lion craving to tear its prey, like a young lion lurking in hiding.
Arise, O LORD; confront him and make him bow down; deliver my soul from the wicked by Your sword,

O LORD, save me by Your hand from such men, from the men of the world whose portion is in this life; as for Your treasure, may their bellies be filled; let their sons be satisfied, and leave abundance to their children.
As for me, I will behold Your face in righteousness; when I awake, I will be satisfied to see Your likeness.
